title: Workflow configuration
description: Configuration required to run either workflow (Qualify a List or Build a List) of the qualitative ICP qualification pattern.

client_name.description: The name of the company running this workflow (used in prompts and, if phase 1 runs, to find its own case studies).

client_website.description: Required only if deriving the qualitative criterion from the client's own case studies (either workflow, run with --derive-criterion).

natural_language_icp.description: The full ICP in plain language, including firmographic and qualitative parts.

qualitative_criterion.description: The specific, checkable qualitative trait that a firmographic schema cannot answer. Must be a concrete statement, not a vague theme. Example: 'operates across multiple legal entities, subsidiaries, or country offices, and is likely still managing spend manually or with fragmented tools per entity'. Example of what NOT to write: 'flexible operations'.

industry.description: Used only by Build a List (discovery). Not required for Qualify a List.

geography.description: Used only by Build a List (discovery). Not required for Qualify a List.

reference_competitor.description: Used only by Build a List (discovery), for the 'alternatives to X' search facet. Not required for Qualify a List.

firmographic_band.description: The target firmographic range used to cross-check candidates in the final phase, independent of the qualitative criterion. Example: '50-1000 employees, headquartered in Europe'.

tier_definition.description: How to assign a tier to each validated company. Must state what separates a strong match from a partial one, and confirm that not_found and firmographically-disqualified companies are excluded, not tiered.

validation_batch_size.description: How many companies to validate per deep-search call in phase 3. 10-15 is the recommended range; see docs/05-pitfalls.md.

then.description: If natural_language_icp is not provided, client_website is required so phase 1 can derive the criterion.
